documents.title: Technical Formulation Brief — Luminance Brightening Serum (Indie Brand: Aura Collective)

sections.heading: Project Overview
sections.content:
Client brand: Aura Collective (indie brand, DTC-first, clean beauty positioning)
Product concept: Lightweight brightening serum with visible dark spot reduction
Target consumer: Women 28–45, hyperpigmentation concerns, clean beauty committed
Product category: Skincare — face serum

sections.heading: Product Specifications
sections.content:
Format/texture: Lightweight water-based serum; fast-absorbing, non-greasy finish
Key actives and target concentrations:
• Niacinamide — 5% (brightening, pore minimizing)
• Tranexamic acid — 3% (dark spot reduction)
• Vitamin C (ascorbyl glucoside, stable form) — 2–3%
• Hyaluronic acid (low and high MW) — 1% combined
Fragrance: Fragrance-free (client hard requirement — clean beauty positioning)
Color: Clear to very slightly pale yellow — no added color
Key claims to support: Reduces appearance of dark spots in 8 weeks, brightens skin tone, minimizes pores

sections.heading: Regulatory & Market Requirements
sections.content:
Target launch markets: United States (primary), Canada (secondary launch, 6 months post-US)
US: FDA MoCRA compliance — cosmetic product; no drug claims; safety substantiation required
Canada: Health Canada Cosmetic Regulations — INCI labeling, bilingual label (English/French)
Claim substantiation required: '8-week dark spot reduction' claim requires clinical or consumer perception study
No market-specific restricted ingredient concerns identified for niacinamide or tranexamic acid at target concentrations — confirm ascorbyl glucoside status in Canada

sections.heading: Packaging Constraints
sections.content:
Primary container: 30mL glass dropper bottle (client has sourced — R&D to confirm compatibility)
Closure: Pipette dropper
Key constraint: Glass packaging — formula must be tested for glass interaction; pH management critical
No aerosol or pressurized packaging

sections.heading: Development Parameters
sections.content:
Target shelf life: 24 months from manufacture date
Target pH: 5.5–6.0 (optimal for niacinamide stability and skin compatibility)
Known exclusions: No parabens, no synthetic fragrance, no mineral oil, no silicones (clean beauty formulation standard)
Benchmark products provided by client: The Ordinary Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1%, Paula's Choice 10% Niacinamide Booster
Client note: Does NOT want to match benchmark exactly — wants a more sophisticated, skin-feel-optimized formula at lower niacinamide concentration with tranexamic acid synergy

sections.heading: Commercial Context
sections.content:
Estimated launch volume: 5,000 units (initial), scaling to 15,000–20,000 units/month by month 6
Target launch date: 6 months from project start
Budget: Development fee budget not disclosed; client is cost-sensitive on COGS — target formula cost under $4.50/unit at 10,000 unit scale
Timeline pressure: Client has a retail pitch in 7 months — formula lock required by week 8

sections.heading: Open Questions for R&D
sections.content:
• Confirm ascorbyl glucoside stability at target pH with niacinamide — niacin flush risk at pH above 6.5
• Assess tranexamic acid supplier options and lead time — confirm availability at required purity
• Confirm glass bottle compatibility — pH and any potential for leaching
• Determine whether 8-week claim requires partnered clinical study or consumer panel — flag to Sales to discuss with client
• Confirm preservative system compatible with clean beauty positioning (no phenoxyethanol preference from client — to confirm)
